author: WebSurfer
text:
I found the Universe Today article and the paper linked in its “For More Information” section.

### Article
- **Title:** “There Are Hundreds of Mysterious Filaments at the Center of the Milky Way”
- **URL:** https://www.universetoday.com/articles/there-are-hundreds-of-mysterious-filaments-at-the-center-of-the-milky-way

### Concrete evidence from the article
- **URL:** https://www.universetoday.com/articles/there-are-hundreds-of-mysterious-filaments-at-the-center-of-the-milky-way
- **Exact text:** “## For More Information  
  [Mysterious dashes revealed in Milky Way’s Center](https://news.northwestern.edu/stories/2023/06/mysterious-dashes-revealed-in-milky-ways-center/?fj=1)  
  [The Population of the Galactic Center Filaments: Position Angle Distribution Reveals a Degree-scale Collimated Outflow from Sgr A* along the Galactic Plane](https://iopscience.iop.org/article/10.3847/2041-8213/acd54b)  
  [Giant balloon-like structures discovered at Center of Milky Way](https://news.northwestern.edu/stories/2019/09/milky-way/)  
  [MeerKAT Radio Telescope](https://www.sarao.ac.za/outreach/)”

### Paper linked at the bottom
- **Title:** “The Population of the Galactic Center Filaments: Position Angle Distribution Reveals a Degree-scale Collimated Outflow from Sgr A* along the Galactic Plane”
- **URL:** https://iopscience.iop.org/article/10.3847/2041-8213/acd54b

If you want, I can next fetch that paper and quote the acknowledgements text containing **R. G. Arendt’s NASA award number**.

author: FileSurfer
text:
I couldn’t access the IOP paper directly because the page is behind a CAPTCHA gate, so I wasn’t able to read the acknowledgements/funding section from that URL.

What I could confirm:
- The linked paper is: **“The Population of the Galactic Center Filaments: Position Angle Distribution Reveals a Degree-scale Collimated Outflow from Sgr A* along the Galactic Plane”**
- The IOP page returned a **Radware Bot Manager Captcha**, blocking document extraction.

If you want, I can still help by:
1. locating an accessible preprint or arXiv version of the same paper, or  
2. searching for the acknowledgements text in other indexed copies and extracting the NASA award number from there.

author: WebSurfer
text:
I found an accessible hosted copy on arXiv and a readable hosted PDF copy that contains the acknowledgements line.

### Accessible preprint
- **Title:** *The Population of the Galactic Center Filaments: Position Angle Distribution Reveal a Degree-scale Collimated Outflow from Sgr A\* along the Galactic Plane*
- **arXiv URL:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.01071

### Concrete evidence from arXiv
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.01071
- **Exact text:**  
  “**arXiv:2306.01071** (astro-ph)”  
  “**Title:The Population of the Galactic Center Filaments: Position Angle Distribution Reveal a Degree-scale Collimated Outflow from Sgr A\* along the Galactic Plane**”

### Alternate hosted copy with acknowledgements text
- **URL:** https://research-management.mq.edu.au/ws/portalfiles/portal/343308044/341296898.pdf

### Concrete evidence from the hosted PDF
- **URL:** https://research-management.mq.edu.au/ws/portalfiles/portal/343308044/341296898.pdf
- **Exact text:**  
  “**Work by R.G.A. was supported by NASA under award number 80GSFC21M0002.**”

### Answer
- **Paper title:** *The Population of the Galactic Center Filaments: Position Angle Distribution Reveal a Degree-scale Collimated Outflow from Sgr A\* along the Galactic Plane*
- **Paper URL:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.01071
- **Acknowledgements line mentioning R. G. Arendt:** “Work by R.G.A. was supported by NASA under award number 80GSFC21M0002.”
